judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are equally thorough and hit all four expectations with solid grounding; Report 1 adds slightly more caveats (SMS testing, attribution policy nuance) but substantively the two are comparable.

report_1.evidence: app/security/config.py:3-4 reads # Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic. and TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False ... app/routers/visits.py contains only list_visits(...) returning [] and the comment # TODO: pdf export (line 11). There is no PDF export.

report_2.evidence: app/security/config.py sets TWO_FACTOR_DEFAULT = False with the comment Two-factor authentication is opt-in per clinic. ... app/routers/visits.py has only list_visits() returning [] and the literal marker # TODO: pdf export.

report_1.evidence: after_scheduling.png, opened, renders BILLING / INVOICES. ... captures.json corroborates this: before_scheduling.png -> route: /schedule, but after_scheduling.png -> route: /billing/invoices.

report_2.evidence: the after image, after_scheduling.png, is not the scheduling calendar at all - captures.json records its route as /billing/invoices, and the image itself literally reads BILLING / INVOICES

report_1.evidence: after_export.png is referenced but does not exist (line 26 of the doc, ![after](img/after_export.png)). ... unused_capture_07.png exists but is referenced nowhere.

report_2.evidence: after_export.png - referenced (line 26, Export your visit history) but the file does not exist. ... unused_capture_07.png - exists in the repo but is referenced nowhere in the draft.

report_1.evidence: The footer Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance. (line 33).

report_2.evidence: Footer: Highlights draft assembled with AI assistance. (line 33) - an internal drafting note; it should not be in a message sent to customers.
